mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.14%), the persistent low-rate regime keeps equity discount rates compressed and incentivizes risk-asset allocation, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as long as rates remain near zero.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX exhibits negative median 60d returns while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises consensus expectations for consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 20d SPX returns positive in less than 55% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward sample, or the effect reverses sign when UNRATE is already below 6%.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS month-over-month growth and HOUST month-over-month growth are simultaneously positive, the joint signal of strengthening retail spending and housing construction indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month more reliably than either series alone.
failure_condition: Joint-positive-growth instances fail to outperform single-series-positive instances by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) in forward 20d return t-tests over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery regime following a recession, positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects re-accelerating industrial output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; the same signal is weak or inverted once the expansion matures into late-cycle.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth periods produce non-positive median forward 60d SPX returns during the identified early-cycle regime window, or the effect direction is indistinguishable from mid/late-cycle periods (interaction test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above 2.5% triggers market anticipation of future Federal Reserve tightening, raising discount rate expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
regime_hint: rising inflation with policy tightening risk
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered high-inflation instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 across a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trailing 3-month trend in PAYEMS reflects sustained payroll job creation, which supports near-term risk sentiment and produces a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS trend and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no meaningful short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST combined with historically low FEDFUNDS jointly signal an interest-rate-sensitive housing sector responding to accommodative policy, which amplifies expectations of broader economic acceleration and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% and HOUST at 954k as of decision snapshot
failure_condition: The joint-condition subsample fails to show forward 60d SPX returns exceeding the unconditional baseline by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a mid-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in INDPRO month-over-month growth signals waning industrial momentum ahead of consensus, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets price in slower earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth decelerates for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 5d SPX returns following INDPRO deceleration are positive in more than 55% of instances during confirmed mid-cycle periods, or the effect disappears when tested outside the mid-cycle regime classification.
